_metadata.notes: Pages 71-79 contain appendices, definitions, appraiser qualifications, and back cover

pages_71_73.content_summary: Legal conditions and disclaimers required by CUSPAP standards, including client authorization, market conditions, legal/environmental/survey responsibilities, court testimony, hidden conditions, and liability limitations
key_points: Report authorization and authorized users only
Appraiser not responsible for legal matters
Market value based on effective date only

pages_74_77.content_summary: Comprehensive glossary of appraisal and real estate industry terminology, organized alphabetically from 'Absorption Period' through 'Value Indication'
key_definitions: Absorption Period/Rate - Time required for property to achieve occupied/leased status
Ad Valorem Tax - Property tax levied in proportion to value
Assessed Value - Government-assigned property value for tax purposes
Authorized Client/Use/User - CUSPAP-defined client and usage permissions
Cash Equivalency - Adjustment for non-market financing terms
Contract Rent - Actual rental income from lease
Disposition Value - Price under forced sale conditions
Effective Rent - Base rent minus landlord concessions
Excess Land - Unneeded land with separate highest/best use
Excess Rent - Contract rent exceeding market rent
Exposure Time - Time property would be on market before sale
Extraordinary Assumption - Assumption that could materially affect conclusions
Fee Simple Estate - Absolute ownership unencumbered
Floor Area Ratio (FAR) - Building floor area to land area ratio
Frictional Vacancy - Vacancy needed for market orderly operation
Full Service Lease - Landlord pays property expenses
General Vacancy - Vacancy calculation in DCF analysis
Going Concern Premise - Business expected to continue indefinitely
Gross Building Area (GBA) - Total floor area measured from exterior
Gross Lease - Landlord receives rent and pays all expenses
Hypothetical Condition - Assumed but untrue condition for analysis
Investment Value - Value to specific investor based on their criteria
Land-to-Building Ratio - Proportion of land to building area
Lease - Legal agreement granting use rights for period
Leased Fee Interest - Owner's right to rent plus residuary right
Leasehold Interest - Lessee's right to occupy and use property
Lessor - One who conveys occupancy rights under lease
Liquidation Value - Price under extreme forced sale conditions
Market Rent - Probable rent property should bring in open market
Market Value - Most probable price in fair competitive sale
Market Value of Going Concern - Market value of business as whole
Marketing Time - Estimated time to sell at market value after effective date
Modified Gross Lease - Landlord and tenant share expense obligations
Net Lease - Tenant pays all property expenses
Net Net Net Lease - Tenant pays all expenses except structural/reserves
Occupancy Rate - Ratio of occupied to total rentable space
Overage Rent - Percentage rent above guaranteed minimum
Percentage Rent - Rent based on percentage of tenant sales
Prospective Opinion - Value opinion for specified future date
Rentable Area - Tenant's usable floor area
Retrospective Value Opinion - Value opinion for historical date
Shelf Rent - Rent for shell space with minimal finishes
Surplus Land - Unneeded land that cannot be sold separately
Turnover Vacancy - Vacancy allowance from unit/suite turnover
Usable Area - Actual occupable area of floor or office
Use Value - Value assuming specific use
Value in Use - Value assuming specific use (equivalent to Use Value)
Value Indication - Conclusion of value from valuation approach

page_78.content_summary: Appraiser professional biography, credentials, and company information

appraiser-credentials.notes: AACI = Accredited Appraiser Canadian Institute, MRICS = Member Royal Institution Chartered Surveyors

appraiser-background.value: Development, brokerage, investment, land sales background with multi-faceted real estate expertise

company-valta-about.value: Provide fair, accurate, and comprehensive valuations for multi-family, apartment, condominium, mixed-use, retail and commercial property

page_79.content_summary: Company contact information and legal disclaimer

legal-disclaimer.value: Information contained herein was obtained from sources deemed reliable and, while thought to be correct, have not been verified. Valta does not guarantee the accuracy or completeness of the information presented. No assumption of responsibility or liability for any errors or omissions therein.

summary.notes: All pages contain standard appendix content required for appraisal reports. Pages 71-73 contain CUSPAP-mandated limiting conditions. Pages 74-77 provide comprehensive glossary. Page 78 profiles the appraisal professional. Page 79 is back cover with company information.
